original: Soviet Union launches Sputnik I, the first artificial Earth satellite, into an elliptical low Earth orbit
SpaceShipOne wins Ansari X Prize for private spaceflight
ABC premiere of Saturday futuristic fantasy cartoon "Thundarr the Barbarian"
Richard Noble driving Thrust2 reaches new land speed record of 650.88 mph (1,047.49 km/h) at Black Rock Desert, Nevada

Pakistan all-rounder Shahid Afridi breaks record for fastest hundred in one-day cricket history; in first ODI innings, Afridi savages Sri Lanka for 102 in KCA Centenary Tournament match in Nairobi; reaches 100 in 37 balls
Dinosaur National Monument in Colorado & Utah is established
Borden's opens a turn-of-century ice cream parlor at Disney World
Typhoon Tip forms near Pohnpei in Micronesia, would go on to be the most intense and largest tropical cyclone then ever recorded
Noble Prize for Physics won by Alain Aspect, John Clauser and Anton Zeilinger for their groundbreaking experiments using entangled quantum states
Chinese pianist Lang Lang plays Carneige Hall one-handed due to injury with 14 year-old Maxim Lando playing his left hand for Gershwin's "Rhapsody in Blue"
Ten thousand people participate in "Rites of Ancestral Return" the reinternment of African American ancestral remains at the African Burial Ground in Manhattan
USSR Luna 3 sent back 1st photos of Moon's far side
Adam Ondracompletes the first ascent of the Change in Norway, the world's first-ever 9b+ (5.15c) route
New York Yankees sluggerAaron JudgebreaksRoger Maris's AL single season HR record when he slams his 62nd homer in a 3-2 loss against the Texas Rangers at Globe Life Field in Arlington, Texas
Nobel Prize for Physiology and Medicine awarded to David Julius and Ardem Patapoutian for discovering receptors for temperature and touch
Three years worth of rain (14 inches or 300 mm) falls in 12 hours in city of Al Khaburah, Oman, in the wake of tropical cyclone Shaheen
Nobel Prize for Chemistry awarded to Moungi Bawendi, Louis Brus and Alexei Ekimov “for the discovery and synthesis of quantum dots”
Gutzon Borglum begins sculpting Mount Rushmore and works on it until 1941
The Orient Express departs on its first official journey from Paris to Istanbul
Chicago Cubs beat Pittsburg Pirates, 4-0 to end MLB season at 116-36 with .763 winning percentage; unmatched since
Soyuz TM-20 launches
Nobel Prize for Chemistry awarded to Jacques Dubochet, Joachim Frank and Richard Henderson for their work on cryo-electron microscopy
Australian motorcycle racer Mick Doohan wins the Australian Grand Prix at Phillip Island to clinch his 5th world 500cc championship; all 5 titles are won with Honda

MLB San Francisco Giants 1st baseman Will Clark hits first NLCS grand slam since 1977 in 11-3 win v Chicago Cubs; Clark goes 4-for-4 with 2 HRs to tie NLCS RBI record (6) for an entire series in less than 4 innings
Patrick Kanemakes his NHL debut with the Chicago Blackhawks against the Minnesota Wild
Kazuki Takahashi, Japanese creator of manga and trading card game Yu-Gi-Oh, born in Tokyo, Japan (d. 2022)
Koji Ishikawa, Japanese children's book author and illustrator, born in Chiba Prefecture, Japan
Kenichi Fukui, Japanese chemist, 1981 Nobel Prize for Chemistry for work on chemical reactions, born in Ikoma, Japan (d. 1998)[1]
Charles Lazarus, American entrepreneur, founder of Toys "R" Us in 1948, born in Washington, D.C. (d. 2018)
Bernice Johnson Reagon, American singer (Freedom Singers; Sweet Honey in the Rock), social activist, educator, and historian, born in Dougherty County, Georgia (d. 2024)[1]
Courier 1B Launched; 1st active repeater satellite in orbit
Omar Ali Saifuddien III of Brunei abdicates in favour of his son, His Majesty Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah

Future Baseball Hall of Fame 3rd basemanMike Schmidt's 2-run homer off Stan Bahnsen in 11th inning gives Philadelphia, 6-4 win v Montreal and NL East title; Schmidt's 48th of the season; breaks Eddie Mathews' single-season MLB record for 3rd basemen
USAF Maj Robert A Rushworth takes X-15 to 32,300m
Last wooden passenger subway cars retired at Brooklyn Myrtle Beach
Kurt Wüthrich, Swiss chemist, 2002 Nobel Prize for developing nuclear magnetic resonance spectroscopy, born in Aarberg, Switzerland[1]
Dutch mathematicianChristiaan Huygenspatents the pocket watch
Frederic Remington, American artist and sculptor of American West, born in Canton, New York (d. 1909)
Mary Two-Axe Earley, Mohawk elder and human rights advocate (fought against women's status under the Indian Act), born on the Kahnawà:ke reserve, Quebec (d. 1996)[1]
Edward Murray East, American botanist and geneticist who developed hybrid corn, born in Du Quoin, Illinois (d. 1938)
Anita Luceete Defrantz, American rower (Olympic bronze 1976; first female vice-president IOC executive committee), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania

Abraham Lincolnresumes his political career with a speech denouncing recent federal legislation extending slavery, at Illinois State Fair in Springfield; event is a precursor to his famous Peoria Speech
Italian Autostrada del Sol opens at Milan-Naples
USSR launches Luna 7; crash lands on Moon

Australian wicketkeeper Ian Healy overtakes countryman Rod Marsh's world record of 355 Test cricket dismissals during 1st Test win v Pakistan in Rawalpindi; catchesWasim Akramoff Colin Miller to reach milestone
Jacqueline Pascal, French child prodigy poet, and nun, born in Clermont-Ferrand, Auvergne, France (d. 1661)
Red Raiders' quarterback B.J. Symons sets a Big 12 Conference record with 8 touchdown passes, leading Texas Tech to a convincing 59-28 win over Texas A & M

Jochen Rindt of Austria posthumously clinches Formula 1 World Drivers Championship at the US Grand Prix at Watkins Glen when contender Jacky Ickx finishes 4th; Rindt killed in a practice accident at the Italian GP at Monza 5/9/70
South Lhonak lake bursts sending flash flooding through India's Lachen valley, in Sikkim state, killing 70 people, with 76 missing, displacing 10,000 people
Dick Tracy comic strip by Chester Gould debuts
TV sitcom "Leave It to Beaver" debuts on CBS
-8] Hurricane Flora, kills 6,000 in Cuba & Haiti
Pier 39 opens in San Francisco
Brendan Shanahan scores a hat trick in Red Wings 4-3 OT win at San Jose; becomes second player in NHL history to score an opening-night hat trick twice in his career
British runnerJim Peterssets world marathon record 2:18:34.8 in the Turku Marathon in Finland
Boston Bruins retire future Hockey Hall of Fame defencemanRay Bourque's #77 jersey in the NHL season opener at Boston Garden; beat visiting Mighty Ducks of Anaheim, 4-2
Future Pro Football Hall of Fame running backJim Brownmakes club record 37 rushing attempts in Cleveland Browns, 34-7 win at Chicago Cardinals
(Francisco) "Chino" Pozo, Cuban-American latin jazz and Afro-Cuban drummer, bongosero and conguero, born in Havana, Cuba (d. 1980)
Johanna van Gogh-Bonger, Dutch editor and wife of Theo van Gogh who promoted Vincent van Gogh's work after his death, born in Amsterdam, Netherlands (d. 1925)
Bill Fagerbakke, American actor (Coach - "Dauber"; SpongeBob - "Patrick Star"), born in Fontana, California
